segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a nighttime exterior shot of a building, prominently featuring a brightly lit blue and white neon sign that reads 'TONY AWARDS', setting a theatrical tone. The scene then transitions indoors to a warmly lit room where a man, identified as James by an unseen voice, is seated on a beige couch. He wears a dark navy zip-up hoodie over a gray turtleneck and dark pants, flanked by patterned throw pillows. The room is adorned with wooden shutters, decorative wall sconces, and abstract artwork. On-screen graphics display 'LIVE' and a 'TV PG L' rating, with the CBS logo in the bottom right, indicating a live broadcast. After a brief moment of applause, James, holding a small black object, initially asks, "How many shows left on my queue?" to which a calm, synthesized female voice responds, "You have 24,601 shows remaining, James. Shall I play them?" He then instructs, "Yeah, play my queue." As the camera slowly zooms in on his reflective face, a dramatic, orchestral musical accompaniment with a theatrical feel begins, and James starts to sing a comedic song about the perils of binge-watching, referencing Netflix, Hulu, and Amazon, and acknowledging network TV as "wonderful, obviously." He gestures subtly with his hands as he delivers the humorous lyrics, maintaining a composed yet engaging demeanor throughout his performance.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a nighttime exterior shot of a building featuring a brightly lit neon sign that reads 'TONY AWARDS' in blue and white, indicating the setting is related to the prestigious theater awards. The scene then transitions to an interior setting, where a man is seated on a beige couch in a warmly lit room. He is wearing a dark navy zip-up hoodie over a gray turtleneck and dark pants. The room has wooden shutters on the windows, decorative wall sconces, and abstract artwork on the wall. Two patterned throw pillows—one with red and orange stripes, the other with beige and brown stripes—flank him on the couch. The man appears to be speaking, occasionally glancing around and gesturing slightly with his hands, which are holding a small black object, possibly a remote or phone. The camera slowly zooms in on him, focusing on his upper body and face as he speaks. On-screen graphics include 'LIVE' in the top left corner and a 'TV PG L' rating, along with the CBS logo in the bottom right, suggesting this is a live broadcast segment. The atmosphere is calm and intimate, with soft lighting and a composed demeanor from the man, who seems to be delivering a personal or reflective message.

audio_updated_entities.Man on Couch: A man with short light brown hair, wearing a dark navy zip-up hoodie over a gray turtleneck and dark pants, seated on a beige couch. He holds a small black object in his hands and speaks with subtle gestures and facial expressions, appearing engaged and reflective. He is identified as 'James' by a female voice and sings a comedic song about binge-watching to an orchestral accompaniment.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of the Man on Couch, a man with short light brown hair wearing a dark navy zip-up hoodie, seated on a plush beige couch adorned with two patterned throw pillows. He holds a small black remote and sings with subtle gestures, his voice carrying a melodic tune about "images of actors who aren't there, playing parts in shows made far away, unfinished, long ago." The warmly lit living room, featuring wooden shutters and Abstract Wall Art, is initially visible, with the CBS Logo and TV PG L Rating consistently displayed. As he continues to sing, "Trade the remote for the near. Leave your couch and travel here," the camera pulls back, and the backdrop dramatically dissolves from the living room into a vast, dimly lit concert hall filled with a massive audience. The couch is now on a stage, and the Man on Couch reacts with surprise as the environment shifts again to a vibrant, neon-lit cityscape with blue vertical lights and illuminated skyscrapers. Enthusiastic audience applause and cheers erupt as he sings, "And experience a different kind of show." A woman in a sparkling gold bodysuit then emerges from behind the couch, followed by two men in white shirts and black suspenders who perform acrobatic moves around the couch. The Man on Couch is lifted and spun by the performers in a choreographed sequence, transforming the scene into a dynamic stage performance, all while he continues to sing about "the kind that's live... actual people in an actual space, watching actors who are actually there."

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on a brightly lit stage with a vibrant Neon Cityscape Backdrop, where an energetic, theatrical performance unfolds to upbeat, show-tune style music featuring a male vocalist singing about the unique, unrepeatable bliss of live performance. The Man on Couch, initially in a dark hoodie, is dynamically lifted and spun by two Men in White Shirts and Suspenders, while a Woman in Gold Bodysuit performs acrobatic splits and flips around the Beige Couch. As the music swells, the Man on Couch is dramatically maneuvered through the air before landing back on the couch and then standing. He swiftly changes into a striking red tuxedo jacket with a black bow tie, transforming into the host. With the CBS Logo visible in the bottom right and the TV PG L Rating in the top left, he confidently walks along a curved stage path, gesturing to the cheering and clapping audience, who are shown in wide shots. The music transitions to a more prominent theme as the host, James Corden, greets the audience, announcing, "Good evening, ladies and gentlemen, and welcome to this. It's the 73rd Annual Tony Awards. I'm your host, James Corden." He begins to introduce a segment but humorously interrupts himself, asking, "Excuse me, Brian, where are you going?" The camera then cuts to a close-up of a Man in a Gray Pinstripe Suit, who appears to be speaking or reacting with expressive hand gestures, possibly a judge or commentator, as the host's question hangs in the air.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a wide shot of a packed theater audience, their attention fixed on a stage illuminated by vibrant blue and white lights, featuring a dynamic neon cityscape backdrop. A man's voice is heard declaring, 'I just have to go to the bathroom,' to which another voice, likely the Man in Gray Pinstripe Suit, urgently responds, 'Now? I mean, the show literally just started. We're in the middle of the opening number!' The first man insists, 'I know, but I I I really have to go,' then dramatically shouts, 'I am bloated as hell! And I'm not gonna take it anymore!' as the audience laughs. The Man in Gray Pinstripe Suit tries to interject, 'Brian! Brian! Brian! You can't!' Suddenly, upbeat show-tune music swells, and the Man in Red Tuxedo Jacket, leading a choreographed dance, begins to sing with a powerful, theatrical voice, 'Guys, we do it live. We go on stage and make it proud for you,' directly referencing the 'Ain't Too Proud' poster. He is joined by the Woman in Gold Bodysuit and the Men in White Shirts and Suspenders, who execute synchronized acrobatic moves around a Beige Couch. The stage is adorned with large, illuminated musical posters for 'Beetlejuice,' 'Tootsie,' 'Hamilton,' and 'The Prom.' The Man in Red Tuxedo Jacket continues to sing, 'The decision comes now. Get ready,' and later, 'I'm gonna live, I'm gonna prepare to shake an extra leg for you,' followed by 'Eight times a week, our actors excel at dancing in heels with a plum,' and 'Eight times a week, we go straight to hell.' He then explicitly references 'The Prom' poster with 'Eight times a week, we go gay to 'The Prom'.' The performers move dynamically across the stage, interacting with the various posters and engaging the audience with high-energy dance moves. The atmosphere is electric, with the audience clapping and cheering throughout the lively and entertaining show. The CBS Logo remains visible in the bottom right corner, and the TV PG L Rating is displayed in the top left.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Man in Red Tuxedo Jacket, now fully transformed, energetically walking across a vibrant stage illuminated by blue and purple lights, surrounded by a dynamic ensemble including the Woman in Gold Bodysuit and Men in White Shirts and Suspenders, all executing synchronized, acrobatic dance moves. An upbeat, theatrical show tune plays, with the Man in Red Tuxedo Jacket singing lyrics like "When we revive, you can't say no to a morning that's unmissable" and "Live, you can't contrive another opening of a show more kissable," emphasizing the theatrical theme. As he leads the choreography with expressive gestures, the stage backdrop shifts between illuminated posters for 'The Prom,' 'Kiss Me, Kate!,' and 'Oklahoma!,' further reinforcing the musical theater context. He dramatically sips from a red-capped water bottle while continuing to dance, and the group performs a high-energy routine with sharp, coordinated steps. The music builds with lyrics "We work for months in advance, 'cause when you see us tonight, we get just one chance." The routine culminates in a formation where the Man in Red Tuxedo Jacket addresses the audience, gesturing with his hands, as the music abruptly stops with a flourish. He then speaks directly, instructing the performers, "That's great everyone, take five! Take five! Take five!" to the sound of audience cheers and applause. He continues, "Well, thanks Sebastian, I appreciate it," and then assesses the performance, stating, "I think it's good. I think it's good, it feels tight. It feels tight, it feels good." He then tries to give further instructions, "Hey, everybody, everybody, attention, attention, listen, listen, just remember next time we do it, just really try and get that very..." before interrupting himself with "Oh, wait." The upbeat music immediately resumes with him singing, "I forgot the performance cannot take a break," as the lively and theatrical atmosphere, enhanced by sparkling stage lights and a neon cityscape backdrop, continues. The CBS Logo and TV PG L Rating remain visible throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with the Man in Red Tuxedo Jacket, clearly the host, walking confidently across the stage as a group of dancers in sparkling gold bodysuits and men in white shirts with suspenders perform dynamic choreography behind him, all set against a vibrant Neon Cityscape Backdrop glowing with blue neon outlines of skyscrapers. The host gestures expressively, engaging the audience with animated hand movements and a charismatic presence, speaking directly in a clear, engaging voice, "Cause this is live. Yeah. You're not watching a signal beaming. None of our shows are streaming. They don't just start anytime. Do you mind, can you? Yeah. We don't charge by a monthly billing. Nobody's Broadway chilling. And no one's on Broadway prime." As the upbeat, theatrical music swells, he is joined by a large ensemble of performers, including the Woman in Gold Bodysuit, now in a shimmering gold sequined gown, and other cast members in formal attire such as tuxedos, a white gown worn by the Woman in White Gown, and a silver suit with a teal bow tie on the Man in Silver Suit with Teal Bow Tie. The group forms a tight, celebratory line, raising their arms in unison, singing in a grand, show-tune style, "We're the theater of a grand tradition. The stage is our noble mission. If you're in our bones. We're much better than television. We view it with great derision. Well, except Game of Thrones. And Fleabag. And Handmaid's Tale. And Killing Eve. And Castle Rock and Walking Dead and Dead..." The host continues to lead with enthusiasm, pointing and speaking to the audience, while the performers around him, including the Man in Black Tuxedo with Bow Tie, the Man with Green Hair, and the Woman in Black Sequined Dress, react with smiles and expressive gestures, all contributing to the energetic, celebratory mood suggesting a finale or curtain call, accompanied by applause and cheers. The CBS Logo remains visible in the bottom right corner, and the TV PG L Rating is displayed in the top left throughout the broadcast.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a vibrant stage performance, featuring a large ensemble in formal and theatrical attire against a neon cityscape backdrop. At the center, the Man in Red Tuxedo Jacket, identified as James Corden, energetically delivers a rapid-fire comedic monologue, listing various TV shows like "Stranger Things," "Orange is the New Black Mirror," and "Marvelous Mrs. Maisel." He gestures expressively, leading the group, while the Woman in White Gown and the Man in Silver Suit with Teal Bow Tie react with wide-eyed surprise and open mouths. The Woman in Gold Bodysuit, now in a shimmering gown, claps and smiles, as do the Man with Green Hair, the Woman in Black Sequined Dress, and the Man in Black Tuxedo with Bow Tie, all displaying animated expressions. Corden concludes his segment by announcing, "And the Late Late Show with me!" to enthusiastic audience applause and cheers. He then promotes his show, stating, "Weeknights at 12:30, right here on CBS, America's most-watched network," prompting more audience laughter and applause. As the camera zooms in, Corden continues his passionate, comedic performance, feigning panic and retracting his previous statements, exclaiming, "Oh my god, what am I doing? What am I saying? I didn't mean anything I just said!" He humorously blames "Audra McDonald" for making him say it, which elicits more laughter. He then professes his love for "TV," explaining, "We all love you, you pay us so much more money than the theater!" The ensemble responds with synchronized gestures and expressions of awe, raising their hands in unison during this dynamic, celebratory moment, all under bright theatrical lighting. The segment concludes with Corden's abrupt, comedic query, "Law and Order corpses?", as the CBS Logo and TV PG L Rating remain visible.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment opens with a wide shot of a stage performance featuring a large ensemble of performers dressed in formal and theatrical attire, standing in front of a vibrant neon cityscape backdrop. At the center is the Man in Red Tuxedo Jacket, who is animatedly speaking and gesturing with expressive hand movements, clearly leading the group. He is flanked by the Woman in White Gown to his left and the Man in Silver Suit with Teal Bow Tie to his right, both reacting with wide-eyed surprise and open mouths. The Woman in Gold Bodysuit, now in a shimmering gold sequined gown, stands prominently on the right side of the group, clapping and smiling. The Man with Green Hair, in a black suit, and the Woman in Black Sequined Dress, in a short black dress with boots, are visible on the far left, both displaying animated expressions. The Man in Black Tuxedo with Bow Tie stands on the far right, smiling and holding a small object. As the camera zooms in, the Man in Red Tuxedo Jacket continues to speak passionately, occasionally looking around at the ensemble, who respond with synchronized gestures and expressions of awe. The group raises their hands in unison at one point, creating a dynamic, celebratory moment. The lighting is bright and theatrical, emphasizing the performers' colorful costumes and the energetic atmosphere. segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of the Man in Red Tuxedo Jacket, a man with short light brown hair in a vibrant red tuxedo, who animatedly speaks to the audience, declaring in a clear, engaging voice, 'Look, let me level with you about Broadway.' His lively tone sets the stage as the scene quickly transitions to a wide shot, revealing him center stage, surrounded by a dynamic ensemble of dancers. This group includes a Woman in Gold Bodysuit and several Men in White Shirts and Suspenders, all performing synchronized choreography against a vibrant Neon Cityscape Backdrop. A fast-paced, show-tune style song with strong brass elements begins, and the host launches into a rapid, humorous song about the drawbacks of Broadway, singing about 'steep' ticket prices, 'terrible' drives, 'crowded and noisy' theaters, and 'limited legroom.' The dancers execute sharp, energetic movements, including coordinated arm gestures and turns, while the host leads with expressive hand motions and facial expressions. Dramatic blue and white neon lighting highlights the performers. As the routine progresses, the dancers form a circle around the host, then turn their backs to the audience in unison, creating a visually striking moment. The music then transitions to a more dramatic, orchestral sound as the host shifts his tone, singing a heartfelt ode to the magic of live performance, describing how it 'will lighten your spirit and gladden your heart,' and how 'the laughs fill your belly, the tears fill your eyes.' He continues to engage with the audience, raising his hands and smiling, maintaining the high-energy atmosphere of this polished and theatrical stage act. The CBS Logo remains visible in the bottom right corner, and the TV PG L Rating is displayed in the top left throughout the segment.

The performance is polished and theatrical, with the host and ensemble working in perfect harmony to deliver a captivating stage act.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of the Man in Red Tuxedo Jacket, a man with short light brown hair in a vibrant red tuxedo jacket, animatedly singing a theatrical, upbeat song about feeling "alive" and "present" on stage against a vibrant Neon Cityscape Backdrop. He gestures expressively as he sings, "your next starts to rise, and it's so indescribably pleasant. You're in the moment and totally present." He is soon joined by a dynamic ensemble of dancers, including the Men in White Shirts and Suspenders and the Woman in Gold Bodysuit, who perform synchronized choreography around him as the song continues, with a chorus joining his vocals. The stage expands to reveal a grand, circular performance area with the TONY AWARDS logo prominently displayed at the center, surrounded by illuminated posters for musicals such as Ain't Too Proud, Beetlejuice, Tootsie, Hamilton, The Prom, Kiss Me, Kate!, and Oklahoma!, reinforcing the Broadway theme of the lyrics. The Man in Red Tuxedo Jacket leads the group in a high-energy dance number, moving across the stage with expressive gestures and vocalizations, singing about how "the show lets you know that you're here, and you're now, and you're real, and you feel alive!" The camera pans to show the audience, including the Man in Gray Pinstripe Suit and the Man in Silver Suit with Teal Bow Tie, who react with excitement and applause, reflecting the song's message about the immersive experience of live theater. The performance crescendos with the entire ensemble dancing in unison, culminating in a wide shot that captures the full scale of the stage, the audience, and the celebratory atmosphere, as the chorus emphatically sings, "Broadway's alive! And that is worthy to be celebrated!" The CBS Logo remains visible in the bottom right corner throughout, and the TV PG L Rating is displayed in the top left.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with an energetic, theatrical musical number at the Tony Awards, featuring a large ensemble in formal and theatrical attire performing a high-energy, synchronized dance routine on a grand stage. The stage is brightly lit with golden drapes, vertical neon light beams, and a prominent 'TONY AWARDS' logo. A male vocalist, identified as the host and the Man in Red Tuxedo Jacket, sings with a powerful, theatrical tenor voice, declaring, "It's time to begin, we're glad you showed up and glad you tuned in." He continues, reflecting on his performance, "As for me, I'll simply say that I... been unsure all along if I get through this song, but I did and it's done. Oh my god, it was fun." During his song, he is lifted and spun by dancers before joining the ensemble, leading them with animated gestures. The Woman in Gold Bodysuit, now in a shimmering gold sequined gown, stands beside him, reacting with wide-eyed enthusiasm to the performance. The ensemble performs choreographed movements, raising arms and striking poses, as the host builds to a crescendo, singing, "It's the Tonys and we are... LIVE!" The music culminates in a grand orchestral flourish as the Man in Red Tuxedo Jacket raises his arms triumphantly. Immediately, the audience in the theater erupts in loud, sustained applause and cheers, which continue for the remainder of the minute. Camera cuts show wide shots of the stage and close-ups of the performers, while audience members like the Man in Gray Pinstripe Suit and the Man in Silver Suit with Teal Bow Tie are seen clapping and cheering, their reactions contributing to the audible excitement. The stage backdrop features illuminated posters for various musicals, including 'Ain't Too Proud,' 'Beetlejuice,' 'Tootsie,' 'Hamilton,' 'The Prom,' 'Kiss Me, Kate!,' and 'Oklahoma!,' enhancing the celebratory atmosphere. The performance concludes with the entire ensemble striking a final pose with raised arms, as the enthusiastic audience applause fills the air. The CBS logo and TV PG L rating remain visible throughout.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a grand stage at the Tony Awards, where a large ensemble of performers in formal and theatrical attire is engaged in a high-energy, synchronized dance routine. The stage is illuminated with golden drapes, vertical neon light beams, and a prominent 'TONY AWARDS' logo at the center. The Man on Couch, now transformed into the Man in Red Tuxedo Jacket, is lifted and spun by dancers before joining the ensemble. He leads the group with animated gestures, his mouth open in song or exclamation, as he moves across the stage. The Woman in Gold Bodysuit, now in a shimmering gold sequined gown, stands beside him, reacting with wide-eyed enthusiasm. The group performs choreographed movements, raising arms and striking poses, while the audience in the theater erupts in applause. The camera cuts between wide shots of the stage and close-ups of the performers, including the Man in Red Tuxedo Jacket, who raises his arms triumphantly. The audience, including the Man in Gray Pinstripe Suit and the Man in Silver Suit with Teal Bow Tie, is shown clapping and cheering. The stage backdrop features illuminated posters for musicals like 'Ain't Too Proud,' 'Beetlejuice,' 'Tootsie,' 'Hamilton,' 'The Prom,' 'Kiss Me, Kate!,' and 'Oklahoma!,' adding to the celebratory atmosphere. The performance culminates in a final pose with the entire ensemble raising their arms, as the audience continues to applaud enthusiastically. The CBS logo remains visible in the bottom right corner throughout, and the TV PG L rating is displayed in the top left.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a grand Tony Awards stage, adorned with a prominent 'TONY AWARDS' neon sign in gold and blue, flanked by elegant drapes and vertical light beams. A large ensemble of performers, dressed in colorful and formal attire, stands together in celebration. The audience, visible in the vast auditorium, is standing and applauding enthusiastically, with many capturing the moment on their phones. Over the sustained, enthusiastic applause, a clear female voice announces, "Ladies and gentlemen, the Tony-nominated book writer of Mean Girls..." The camera slowly zooms out, revealing the full scale of the venue and the energetic crowd, as the applause continues. The scene then transitions to a promotional graphic for the '73rd Annual Tony Awards,' featuring a man in a navy blue tuxedo with a black bow tie, pointing upward against a soft blue gradient background with sparkling light effects. Text on the right side reads 'Watch the Entire Show on CBS.COM and CBS ALL ACCESS.' The CBS logo is visible in the bottom right corner, and the 'TV PG L' rating appears in the top left corner throughout the segment, as the applause gradually fades out.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a grand stage at the Tony Awards, where a large ensemble of performers, dressed in a variety of colorful and formal attire, stands together in celebration. The stage is adorned with a prominent 'TONY AWARDS' neon sign in gold and blue, flanked by elegant golden drapes and vertical light beams, creating a glamorous and celebratory atmosphere. The audience, seated in a vast auditorium, is shown standing and applauding enthusiastically, with many capturing the moment on their phones. The camera slowly zooms out, revealing the full scale of the venue and the energy of the crowd. The scene then transitions to a promotional graphic for the '73rd Annual Tony Awards,' featuring a man in a navy blue tuxedo with a black bow tie, pointing upward with his right index finger. The background is a soft blue gradient with sparkling light effects, and text on the right side reads 'Watch the Entire Show on CBS.COM and CBS ALL ACCESS.' The CBS logo is visible in the bottom right corner throughout the segment, and the 'TV PG L' rating appears in the top left corner, indicating the broadcast content rating. The overall tone shifts from live event excitement to a polished, promotional conclusion.
